products.menuLine: Mount Gay XO · Barbados · $45 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: banana, vanilla

guidance.bestFor: Rum curious beyond Diplomatico, elegant cigar, tropical fruit.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants overproof or agricole only.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when rum should be elegant not syrupy — banana, vanilla, and polished oak.
guidance.memberBlurb: Mount Gay XO — banana and vanilla from Barbados in an elegant rum. Refined where Diplomatico is decadent.

products.menuLine: New Amsterdam Vodka · California · $15 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: citrus, clean grain

guidance.bestFor: House vodka, citrus cocktails, budget-conscious member.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the lounge needs an approachable house vodka — citrus lift without Grey Goose pricing.
guidance.memberBlurb: New Amsterdam — citrus-leaning grain vodka in the value tier America actually buys. Fine for highballs beside a mild smoke.

products.menuLine: Nikka Coffey Grain Whisky · Japan · $65 · 90 proof (45.0% ABV) · Notes: tropical fruit, caramel

guidance.bestFor: Sweet whisky fan, Japanese curious, mild cigar.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when approachable Japanese sweetness matters — tropical fruit and caramel.
guidance.memberBlurb: Nikka Coffey Grain — tropical sweetness from a column still. Japanese whisky that is easy to love when malts are impossible to find.

products.menuLine: Nikka From the Barrel · Japan · $55 · 102.8 proof (51.4% ABV) · Notes: citrus, spice

guidance.bestFor: Japanese whisky fan, cask strength, bold cigar.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when Japanese whisky should hit hard — citrus, spice, and 100+ proof balance.
guidance.memberBlurb: Nikka From the Barrel — Japanese whisky at cask strength. Citrus and pepper in a pour enthusiasts hunt when Yamazaki is gone.

products.menuLine: Oban 14 Year · West Highland, Scotland · $85 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: orange peel, honey

guidance.bestFor: Balanced Scotch, medium-full cigar, coastal character.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants one Scotch that does fruit and light smoke — orange, honey, sea salt.
guidance.memberBlurb: Oban 14 — orange and honey with a touch of West Highland smoke and sea. One glass that covers fruit and peat without extremes.

products.menuLine: Old Forester 1920 Prohibition Style · Kentucky · $65 · 115 proof (57.5% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: dark cherry, chocolate

guidance.bestFor: Experienced bourbon drinker, maduro pairing, user wants proof and dark fruit.
guidance.avoidIf: Beginner, empty stomach, or user wants mild Connecticut-only pairing.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants a stronger bourbon for maduro — dark cherry, chocolate, and controlled heat.
guidance.memberBlurb: Old Forester 1920 — cherry preserves and caramel over dark chocolate, maple, and toasted coconut at 115 proof. Distillery-official maduro partner.

products.menuLine: Patrón Silver · Jalisco, Mexico · $45 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: fresh agave, citrus

guidance.bestFor: Blanco request, margarita, clean pairing with mild cigar.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants crisp agave — citrus, pepper, and mineral.
guidance.memberBlurb: Patrón Silver — clean agave and citrus in a blanco everyone recognizes. The tequila pour for margaritas and mild cigars alike.

products.menuLine: Penelope Bourbon · Kentucky / Indiana · $42 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: caramel, vanilla

guidance.bestFor: Younger member, trendy bourbon ask, medium cigar.
guidance.avoidIf: Purist who rejects sourced whiskey only.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member asks for Penelope or wants modern approachable bourbon.
guidance.memberBlurb: Penelope Bourbon — caramel and orchard fruit in a modern pour lounges actually stock. Approachable premium without the allocated chase.
